item.title: Colombia Unexpectedly Halts Interest Rate Rises Amid Dispute
item.description: Colombia’s central bank unexpectedly halted a series of interest rate increases that had provoked a bitter clash with the government.

item.title: The Audio Industry Is Grappling with the Rise of ‘Podslop’
item.description: Over the past nine days, 39% of new podcasts were likely AI-generated, according to the Podcast Index.

item.title: AI Debt Investors Show Signs of Fatigue After $300 Billion Binge
item.description: After a $300 billion AI debt binge that spanned every corner of the credit market, investors are starting to show some signs of fatigue.

item.title: US Critical Mineral Inventory Plan Includes Buying China Metals
item.description: The US Export-Import Bank’s proposed stockpiling initiative would initially source critical minerals from anywhere in the world, including China, an official involved in the project has revealed.

item.title: Bitcoin Faithful Came to Las Vegas But the Rally Didn’t Show Up
item.description: For a few days every year, the world’s most fervent Bitcoin believers descend together in orange cowboy hats, ties and T-shirts to chant about $1 million coins. This year, in Las Vegas, the chants worked on everyone — except the market.

item.title: Kalshi Bows to Agriculture Industry Pushback on Trading Hours
item.description: Kalshi Inc. has agreed to limit trading hours on new financial contracts tied to crops like corn and wheat after facing pushback from the agriculture industry and derivatives exchanges.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Backs Housing Access Plan as Election Nears
item.description: Giorgia Meloni’s government presented a series of measures aimed at expanding housing access for Italians, part of a push to address cost-of-living concerns as the prime minister prepares for a general election in 2027.

item.title: KKR Preparing New $10 Billion AI Firm Led by Ex-Amazon Web Chief
item.description: KKR & Co. has secured more than $10 billion to launch a company that will develop and operate artificial intelligence infrastructure, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Dollar Wraps Worst Month Since June as Haven Trade Unwinds
item.description: The US dollar is heading for its worst monthly slump since June as the promise of peace talks to end the Iran war led traders to unravel bets on the haven currency.

item.title: French Government Keeps Growth Forecast Despite Stagnation
item.description: France’s government is not revising its growth forecasts despite a surprise stagnation in the first quarter on weak domestic demand and a fall in exports, Finance Minister Roland Lescure said Thursday.

item.title: Citi Retreats From Physical Metals Markets as Bank Cuts Traders
item.description: Citigroup Inc. is stepping back from physical trading in industrial metals and has made a number of staff from its commodities team redundant, an employment tribunal in London heard.

item.title: Musk Says No Written Agreement for His Early Donation to OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk acknowledged there was no written agreement or contract with OpenAI regarding the terms of his donation to the company when it was first founded as a nonprofit research organization more than a decade ago.

item.title: Why Anthropic’s Mythos Is Sparking Global Alarm
item.description: Anthropic PBC has said its new artificial intelligence tool, Mythos, is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can’t be released to the general public. The AI giant released Mythos to a limited number of carefully chosen parties because if a tool this powerful fell into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could help attackers more easily steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure.

item.title: ‘Right Tail Risk’ Trade Put to Test by Megacap Earnings Deluge
item.description: With the US-Iran standoff keeping energy prices at levels that threaten the economy, investors have plenty of risks to worry about when analyzing possible outcomes that could damage portfolios.

item.title: Novo-Backed Drugmaker Avalyn Soars 44% After $300 Million US IPO
item.description: Avalyn Pharma Inc. shares jumped 44% in its trading debut, after the biopharmaceutical company raised $300 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

item.title: ECB and BOE Edge Toward Rate Hikes as Energy Turmoil Deepens
item.description: As oil prices briefly came within reach of $130 a barrel, the mood music among central bankers in Frankfurt and London shifted in a more hawkish direction on Thursday.

item.title: The Haves and Have Nots of AI
item.description: The world’s already fracturing along technological fault lines.

item.title: AI Boom Boosts US Economy, Offering Way to Blunt War Impact
item.description: The AI growth engine was on full display in the first quarter, powering the US economy through fresh headwinds from a war-driven surge in inflation.

item.title: Audi F1 CEO Tries to Turn Also-Ran Legacy Team Into a Winner
item.description: Audi’s Formula 1 boss said entering the sport via a legacy team has proved more difficult than starting with a clean slate, as the German carmaker runs up against more established rivals.

item.title: Hospitals Sell Muni Debt at Fastest Pace in More Than a Decade
item.description: Hospitals across the country are bracing for financial pain ahead of sweeping Medicaid cuts from the Trump administration. But that hasn’t stopped them from borrowing at the fastest pace in more than a decade.

item.title: OpenAI, Meta Targeted in AI Child Safety Bill Senate Panel Backs
item.description: A powerful Senate committee backed legislation to require OpenAI, Meta Platforms Inc. and other artificial intelligence companies to stop minors from using chatbots, responding to a strengthening public backlash against harm to children and teenagers attributed to the fast-spreading technology.

item.title: US Mortgage Rates Jump to 6.3% With First Increase in a Month
item.description: US mortgage rates rose for the first time in a month, with the key spring selling season slow to gain momentum.

item.title: Pemex Posts Third Straight Loss Despite War-Fueled Oil Rally
item.description: Petroleos Mexicanos posted its third straight quarterly loss as surging global oil prices failed to boost the debt-laden oil giant’s finances amid slumping production and refineries that are bleeding cash.

item.title: Euro-Area Finance Chiefs to Discuss Mythos as Concerns Mount
item.description: Euro-area finance ministers will discuss the challenges that Anthropic PBC’s Mythos AI model poses as officials struggle to get clarity on the new tool’s powers.

item.title: New York Life Eyes Potential Canadian Dollar Debt Sale
item.description: New York Life Global Funding is weighing a Canadian-dollar debt offering as soon as Thursday, according to people familiar with the matter, who asked not to be identified as they aren’t authorized to speak publicly.

item.title: Jana Pushes Markel to Divest Ventures, Buy Back $2 Billion Shares
item.description: Jana Partners is renewing its push for Markel Group Inc. to divest its venture arm while pushing the insurer to also buy back $2 billion of its shares to boost the stock.

item.title: ECB Officials See June Hike If Energy Prices Don’t Ease First
item.description: European Central Bank policymakers are likely to raise interest rates at their next meeting in June unless there are positive developments on energy prices and ending the Iran war, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: AI Hallucinations Put South African Ministers on the Spot
item.description: South Africa’s Democratic Alliance party has extolled the need to adopt modern technology to boost government efficiency since joining the ruling coalition as the second-biggest party in 2024.

item.title: Copper and Silver Miner Lumina Shares Slip After Toronto IPO
item.description: Lumina Metals Corp. shares fell 2% after the Canadian company and one of its shareholders raised C$406.2 million ($297 million) in an initial public offering.

item.title: Banamex Is Selling Global Bonds for First Time Since Citi Sale
item.description: Mexico’s Grupo Financiero Banamex is tapping global markets for the first time since Citigroup Inc. started offloading its stake in the bank.
